title: Talking About 24 hours of Moab Solo Mountain Bike Race 2010 - 1stx 3/4

transcript.text: So I was like, whoopsies, so I get up, I go, and I see her over in the pit, and I'm like, okay, and I run over to my pit, oh, come on, you freaking thing, man, don't slide down, Jesus Christ, it's like, where's my, where's my, uh, my tripod, I had it just, like, a couple minutes ago, let's make sure that my face is in here, oh, look, there's plenty of light, too, that's good,

Okay, so, um, oh, I hope there's enough time, um, I, uh, so distracting when I have to see my face, though, so I went in, and they were, like, wanting me to hang out, and my mom, actually, on a lot of those laps wanted me to hang out for a long time, and I was like, mom, I told you not to say that to me, don't fucking say that, don't make me try to sit down,

Like, and she did, was doing some other annoying shit, too, like, she was, like, taking my electrolyte shot blocks and putting them in another place, I was like, at one point, I poured out my entire backpack, I'm like, where the fuck are they, she's like, well, what are, what are you talking about, the shot blocks, oh, I put them over here, like, don't do that, you know, they have to be where I need to find them,

Um, so, anyways, um, I, um, I go in there, and they gave me, like, a big sandwich and a lot of potatoes and stuff, and that was the longest, actually, pit I had, because they had a lot of food, and I just wanted a lot of food, and I was starting to get really tired from not being able to breathe, and, and that's, anyways, but I kept, I kept my eye on the trail to make sure she, that girl didn't go by, because I could recognize, usually, because she had this, like, yellow honey stinger jacket on, jersey on,

So then I went, and, uh, like, in the beginning of the lap, after my pit, I was, like, zero leg speed, I was going, like, five miles an hour, and then, like, Kelly Miguelke passes me, and he's, like, the guy who got second, he's, like, good job, my friend, only one lap left, I guess you could, like, notice that I was, like, going really slow and hurting and stuff, or going slow, and so then they thought that was hurting, and I think, Josh Tostado, when he passed me, he was, like, behind me for a little bit on some of the really technical stuff, when he passed me, he was, like,

Nice job, 19, so I was pretty good on those technical stuff, but anyways, um, yeah, but, like, people, they're really nice, they're, like, really encouraging, especially when they notice that you're a solo rider, because, like, solo rider have, like, on the, on the, they have, like, a color, like a, I thought, like, an orange color above the number, I never, like, saw a solo rider looking at the number, every time I looked at a number, I saw a normal, uh, number, but there were, the, I did notice some other solo riders, like, some guy was, like, you solo, I was, like, yeah, I was, like, you solo, he's, like, yeah,

I was, I was, like, well, I was, like, what lap are you on, 12, and I was, like, oh, I'm on 11, so that guy had lap me, and then some other guy, some other, like, young, dude on a single speed, for some reason, I was, like, I was, like, are you, I think he said, like, are you solo, and I was, like, yeah, are you solo, he's, like, yeah, I was, like, wow, nice job, because he's on a single speed, passing me,

Because they have another, uh, another category, single speed, um, bikes, which I didn't get, see, I didn't, like, I had been out of the loop, and, until, like, a couple, like, a month beforehand, when I was in Norway, with my buddy, going, like, oh, man, I want a single speed mountain bike, I didn't even know they existed,

And I didn't even know, like, single speed, I mean, I didn't know that 29 inch mountain bikes existed, and I didn't know that single speeds existed either, you know, until this guy who I was biking with a couple weeks beforehand was, like, telling me about it, and, like, why people do them, and that, like, they're really not that much slower than normal bikes, like, and the pros, like, if a pro does 14 laps, 15 laps, then, like, the single speed pro will do 14, and because they're lighter, and, um, you know, and it works somehow, but, and when I saw that, um, I was, like,

It's interesting, I mean, going up, those guys could seem like a pretty good gear, I saw one girl on a single speed, I think there were just as many girl soloists on single speeds as non-single speeds, and she was, like, cranking it, like, so slow, I'm still on the bike, going up the steep part, and, so I could never be a single speed guy on an endurance ride, I would blow my knee out on the first, like, two hours, but, um, but anyways, I understand how single speeds, um,
Um, the logic behind single speeds, but I should talk about that later, I should finish the actual description of the race, um, so I was, like, on the last lap, and I was, like, in so much pain
From having to, like, mental pain, from having to, like, go, for, like, the last three hours, and I had two more hours to do it, to have to do it like that,

And I was, like, you know, on the granny gear, just trying to keep the bike upright, so I wouldn't have to walk it, because that would make me really slow, and then I went, and, but then I, so I let go of the bike, the brakes as I went downhill, and my weight would help me go really fast, and then the momentum would get me up the golly for a little bit, and I actually did okay,

Okay, and then I got off, um, and I took, like, five hits of the inhaler, I, like, didn't fuck around, I was, like, like, waited till I went out of breath, which took me forever, and then I went, you know, I made sure to get it in, but I didn't do shit, um,

So then I went through, and then I went, and then by the time I got to the flats, I was, like, okay, good, the hard stuff's over, but I was, like, I was gone at that point, I was, I was done, I didn't want to do another lap, and I knew I wasn't going to have the time anyways,

And all these peace pullers zooming by me, like, like, you know, this is your last lap, like, they're really, really, um, really supportive, and people who passed, you know, they would notice you're a solo rider, and they're, like, nice going solo, man, badass, and at one point at night, some guys, like,

I was, like, oh, you're solo, oh, cool, these are the hell hours, and I was, like, really, does it get easier in the day, he's, like, fuck, yeah, it gets easier in the day, once the day breaks, it's just over all of a sudden, and it actually kind of happened like that, I only got to do two laps during the day, after the sunrise, because I was going so slow, but, um, and then, like, one girl, Pat, is, like, riding behind me, for, like, a long time,

And she's, like, hey, like, want to chat me up, too, like, where are you from, what's your name, she's, like, I was, like, hi, I was, like, my name's Laurie, I was, like, nice to meet you, nice to meet you, too, see you at the end, and then she, like, zooms off,

And, oh, but I saw that short girl pass me three times, just zoom by me, oh, and then on my 11th lap, I got lapped by Yari Kirkland, the chick who won, at the same exact place that she passed me on my third lap,

Um, and, um, she said the same thing, nice job, 19, just, like, looking forward, like, just, like, concentrated looking her eye, and, uh, yeah, so then I'm going down the backside, and I'm, like, barely moving, I'm, like, going like this, unlike the flats, and people are passing me, like, dude, are you okay, I'm, like, uh, like, are you sure, and I'm, like, uh, uh, because I couldn't say

Anything, I didn't have any breath, I was going, and then I got to the climb, and I was doing granny gear, and then at one point on the climb, I got off the bike, and it wasn't even, like, a technical section, I just got off the bike, because it, because it was, it just got a little bit steaver, and I walked for a little bit, I shouldn't have done that, that was me just being, like, weak, but then I walked over this one technical section, and then I, and then I was, like, almost at the top, and I was starting to pedal, and starting to, like, ride as hard as I could again, because I knew I was almost there,

And, you know, I did get off my bike the first time, because I thought that girl wasn't going to do another lap, that girl, Chris Cannon, wasn't going to do another lap, I figured she would have passed me by now, um, so I kind of, I wasn't worried about, at that point, I wasn't worried about riding as hard as I could, I was just in it for survival, and just, you know, it was, physically, it wasn't as bad as, like, bonking, but mentally, it was almost as bad as bonking, actually,

No, it was about as bad as bonking, but mentally, I was, like, I was so sick of it, man, and I was thinking about, like, am I going to die of a heart attack, like, riding as hard as I can with no oxygen, you know, but anyways, I get, right when I get to the top of the, of the, of the, of the climb, she passes me, like, I'm standing still, and I look over, and I go, out loud, I go, oh, shit,

Shit, but then I put it in my lowest gear, because I was in the top, and I was like, alright, this is the race, right here, and I just start sprinting, out of the saddle, on this full suspension bike, like, that's going, eh, eh, eh, eh, eh, eh, eh, I didn't have it on the, on the hardest setting, you have three settings, I had it in the middle, I should have put it on the super hard, yeah, but I went through, and my weight let me go really fast, it's a fast downhill, um, carried me through the sand and stuff,

And then I got to the bottom, but then at the bottom of the gully, I had to do, like, a climb that was, like, a half, that was, like, a half mile, but I, at the bottom, I looked back, I didn't see her anywhere in sight, but I, like, sprinted up that hill as hard as I could, breathing like this, so I wasn't really going that fast, but, and then I went down, I'm going as hard as I can, which wasn't really hard, and then, right when I entered the chute, I see my brother there, and then I turn around the chute, and I look, and that girl is right behind me,

And if that chute had been, like, 20 feet farther, she would have passed me, and she would have beat me, so I went around, and I went, and I took my thing out, and I slapped it really fast, so I was like, oh, God, I beat her, oh, thank God, and then she, like, zooms past me, slaps it, and she, I don't think she noticed me at all, but maybe she was trying to pass me, I don't know, some, I don't know how the fuck she got from being nowhere in sight to being on my ass

Within, like, a mile or something of, of riding, of, like, a half hour, half mile long climb, or, or maybe it was only, like, a quarter mile long, no, it was, like, a half mile long climb, anyways, but I was, I went into the chute, I saw my brother filming me,

And, and, that was cool, and then he filmed me stop, and then, yeah, they filmed me as I walked back, and, um, I just wanted to lie down, and I couldn't, I couldn't breathe, but I wanted to get my breath back,

And, and then, um, like, I, like, 15 minutes after I was done, like, my brother took my shoes off, my mom took my pulse, and it was still at 100, and, um, and then, uh, I sat up, and I started hacking up all of these, like, giant, like, this big, you know, like, quarter-sized chunks of, like, mustard-colored with, like, little black specks in it, because it was really hard on your lungs, like, at night, I could see my light shining through, dust particles,

That you can't see in the day, but they're all over the place, and you can see them at night, and then there was all these fires, and you could, and you were inhaling the fire smoke as you're going through, which is really rough on the lungs, too, so those little black specks were probably, like, ash, but it was, like, there was, like, this, the thickest, and the biggest, and the thickest consistency thing I'd ever seen, it was, like, a booger, a giant booger, this big, and I hacked up, like, a half dozen of those, and then I hacked up, like, another dozen, like, smaller ones,

Just, like, spitting them into a paper towel and throwing them down, and then after, like, 10 or 15 minutes of doing that, I could, I could, the asthma attack went from category 10 to kind of, like, a, just a, an asthma attack that wasn't, like, completely annoying, like, hell on earth, like, those kind of asthma attacks, even if you're not writing, are hell on earth, like, I managed to write it, I guess, just from adrenaline
Where the inhaler doesn't do anything, and you're going, and you're, like, you feel like you're suffocating, like, you're going to die, at one point, I thought I was going to die,
In Chile, I really did almost pass out, but, um, that's when I vowed never to smoke weed again, you know, and then I go a year without smoking, and then I'd smoke again,
But anyways, after, like, 15 minutes, I hacked up enough of that to be able to have, like, a manageable asthma, and that might have been a good idea, like, if that ever happens again,
Well, it won't happen again, but, if that were to happen again, I would, it would be a good idea to just take 20 minutes off,
And just hack that shit out, and then go again, and you could make your time up, but, um, anyways, next time I do a race like that,
Even if it's only, like, a short distance race, mountain bike race, I'm going to wear a mask the entire time,
So that I don't, so that I don't get any asthma at all, because it'll save time that way, and I was, like, I put on a mask today,
And I was breathing on it, and it didn't seem like it's really, it hinders you any, really that much,
So I have a prediction, like, in 20 years, everybody's going to be wearing those masks,
Everybody's going to realize that, that, that's, that you're going to perform a lot better if you can
Breathe, and maybe people have asthma attacks, and they don't realize it, but I don't know,
But, um, anyways, I'm going to do that, um, so, yeah, I ended up getting the 16th out of 44 guys,
Um, it was funny, I came through one lap, like, a night lap, and the race director goes,
Oh, look, and here's Kyle Pounds, and look what he wrote on his thing, and I wrote,
I have to win this race at all costs, like, like, um, what did I write, I absolutely have to win
This race, so that I can get sponsorship, and get a modern bike, I want a goddamn modern bike,
One of these 29 inch full suspension bikes, don't worry, I won't, like, try and run anybody off the
Road, because I don't even know who my competition is, I guess he thought that was funny, and then I
Took off, and then, but, um, I wrote that because I thought that amateur category was a separate
Category, but then when I was racing, and they were telling me what place I was, I was 14th for
The whole race, until I dropped two places after the asthma, it turns out that I was in the same
Category as the championship, so, I was in 14th, and I went to 16th, so maybe, I know I had the second
Fastest time of the for fun guys, so maybe I want to find out what place I got for the for fun guys,
Because that's what I meant to say, was that I wanted to win the for fun category, but, um, which
Is had more guys, had 29 guys, and then progress class had 17 guys, but, um,
Yeah, so I stopped, and my brother wanted to take off right away, so I did, they just set
Up the car and drove home, and back to the hotel, and, uh, we didn't stop and do the,
Like, uh, reward ceremony, whoops, I gotta stop this, okay.
